tasks_completed.description: Split files_modified vs files_created derivation in agents/dev.md Git-diff derivation paragraph
tasks_completed.changes: Replaced single-rule 'git diff --name-only <baseline_head_sha> for both files_modified and files_created' with split rule: files_modified via git diff --name-only, files_created via git ls-files --others --exclude-standard (excluding paths in baseline_dirty_snapshot). Graceful degradation clause preserved.
tasks_completed.rationale: git diff --name-only cannot enumerate untracked new files. QA (from iter2) already checks files_created via ls-files; this aligns the derivation rule with the check method. Addresses F1 from ba-qa-report iter2.

tasks_completed.description: Split Phase 2 provenance filter in agents/changelog-analyst.md to use ls-files for files_created subset
tasks_completed.changes: Updated provenance filter step 3 from single git diff check covering both files_modified and files_created to a split filter: files_modified checked via git diff --name-only <baseline_head_sha>, files_created checked via git ls-files --others --exclude-standard. Updated step 5 warning log message to distinguish source (git diff vs ls-files) per path set. Also reverted unintended pre-existing working-tree change at line 62 (NESTED_REPO diagnostic text) to match baseline.
tasks_completed.rationale: New untracked files do not appear in git diff --name-only, so using git diff for files_created always falsely classifies legitimate new files as provenance_anomaly. Addresses F2 from ba-qa-report iter2. Codex finding 3 (warning message inaccuracy) also fixed.

git_rationale.root_cause_commit: N/A — spec omission, not a regression from a specific commit
git_rationale.why_issue_occurred: iter2 fixed the QA CHECK side (M4/AC3) to validate files_created via git ls-files, but left the DERIVATION side (agents/dev.md) and the CHANGELOG-ANALYST side using git diff for files_created. This created an internal inconsistency: QA checks files_created correctly while dev is told to populate it incorrectly.
git_rationale.how_fix_addresses_root: Aligns derivation (agents/dev.md) and changelog-analyst provenance filter with the already-correct QA check method: ls-files for files_created, git diff for files_modified.

diff_stats.justification_for_overage: The split rule in agents/dev.md replaces a single-line mandate with a 3-bullet block (header + 2 bullets). The changelog-analyst.md provenance filter step 3 is split from 1 line into 3 lines, plus step 5 log message extended. Net expansion is inherent to expressing a split rule; no extraneous content added.

dev.qa_notes: Verify: (1) agents/dev.md Git-diff derivation paragraph specifies ls-files --others --exclude-standard for files_created; (2) agents/changelog-analyst.md step 3 uses split filter; (3) agents/changelog-analyst.md step 5 warning message correctly distinguishes files_modified (git diff) from files_created (ls-files); (4) agents/qa.md NOT modified (already correct from iter2); (5) commands/dev.md NOT modified (already correct from iter1); (6) agents/changelog-analyst.md line 62 NESTED_REPO text matches baseline (no unintended drift).

self_verification.notes: Round 3: Pure markdown-only changes. git diff confirms only agents/dev.md and agents/changelog-analyst.md changed vs baseline (2 files, 30 insertions, 0 deletions). agents/changelog-analyst.md line 62 unintended drift caught by Codex finding 1 and reverted. No build system for markdown-only changes.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: 7 findings: (1) BLOCKER — unintended scope drift at agents/changelog-analyst.md:62 (NESTED_REPO text shortened — pre-existing working tree change). PROPOSED_FIX: revert. (2) BLOCKER — files_created rule misses staged new files. PROPOSED_FIX: union ls-files with git diff --diff-filter=A. (3) BLOCKER — step 5 anomaly warning inaccurate for files_created. PROPOSED_FIX: split warning by source set. (4) MAJOR — baseline_dirty_snapshot comparison underspecified. (5) MAJOR — parallel dev aggregate drops new provenance fields. (6) MINOR — unborn-repo fallback vague. (7) OBSERVATION_ONLY — split-rule direction is correct.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Finding 1 applied: reverted agents/changelog-analyst.md:62 to baseline NESTED_REPO text. Finding 3 applied: updated step 5 warning log to distinguish source (git diff for files_modified, ls-files for files_created). Finding 2 rejected: staged-file edge case is out of scope for iter3 — BA spec F1/F2 explicitly mandate ls-files for files_created; adding --diff-filter=A requires new BA iteration. Finding 4 rejected: commands/dev.md baseline capture is out of scope per dispatch. Finding 5 rejected: commands/dev.md parallel aggregate is out of scope per dispatch. Finding 6 rejected: unborn-repo fallback refinement is out of scope.

recommendations: Finding 2 (staged-file edge case): if dev agents are permitted to stage files during implementation, BA should update the derivation rule to union git ls-files --others --exclude-standard with git diff --name-only --diff-filter=A <baseline_head_sha> for files_created.

Finding 4 (baseline_dirty_snapshot parsing): BA should specify how changelog-analyst and QA should parse the raw porcelain text into a path list (strip status codes, handle quoted paths, handle directory-level ?? entries).

Finding 5 (parallel aggregate): commands/dev.md parallel aggregate construction rule should be updated to include baseline_head_sha, baseline_dirty_snapshot, and dev.observed_preexisting in the union/copy logic.
